title: Yield Trap
intro: InvITs can advertise striking yields. Sort each card into whether it supports a naive ‘high income like an FD’ reading — or warns that headline yield hides structure.

cards.text: Advertised annual yield of 12–20% vs bank FD rates
Payout may include return of capital, not only earnings
Steady cash distributions highlighted in product notes
Concession length and asset life bound future cash flows
Unit price can fall even while distributions continue
‘Attractive alternative to equity in a choppy market’ headline
Counterparty and tariff/toll contract terms drive risk
Fees and trust structure sit between asset cash and your pocket

round2.text: Distribution yield quoted before fees and expenses
NAV can drop while distributions look steady
Toll/tariff reset clauses buried in offer docs
Compared only to FD rates in the brochure

debrief.summary: Headline InvIT yields are a starting clue, not the ending answer. Separate distribution theatre from what you keep after structure, capital return, fees, and unit-price risk.
